raga.name: Desh
raga.description: The raga of the monsoon and patriotic songs. Light, romantic, and playful — a favourite for film music and thumri. Uses komal Ni in descent and shuddh Ni in ascent in some interpretations, but the primary form uses komal Ni throughout. Associated with rain, longing, and homecoming.

forbidden.name: Tivra Madhyam
forbidden.reason: Desh uses shuddh Ma only. Tivra Ma would push it towards Yaman territory.

forbidden.name: Shuddh Nishad
forbidden.reason: In the primary Desh form, komal Ni is used. Shuddh Ni belongs to Bilaval thaat.

movement.aroha: S R G m P D n S'
movement.avaroha: S' n D P m G R S
movement.pakad_string: R G m P, D n S', n D P m G

characteristic_phrases.context: Upper register — the komal Ni to Sa movement is signature Desh
Descending phrase — the komal Ni lends a wistful quality

characteristic_phrases.phrase: G m P D n S'
characteristic_phrases.context: Full ascending phrase — captures the joyful, expansive Desh spirit

note_rules.Sa_approach: Approached from Re above or komal Ni below — both are natural and characteristic
note_rules.komal_Ni: The defining colour of Desh. The b7 gives it a Mixolydian flavour — bluesy, warm, slightly yearning
note_rules.Ga_role: Bright and prominent — gives Desh its major-key optimism despite the komal Ni
note_rules.D_role: Important in the upper register — D n S' is a signature Desh cadence

vadi.role: King note — Re carries the raga's gentle, romantic character. Receives sustained treatment and emphasis.

samvadi.role: Queen note — Pa anchors the upper register. The Re-Pa axis (a perfect 4th apart) provides structural balance.

context.rasa: Shringara (romance) and Hasya (joy) with undertones of Viraha (separation)
context.aesthetic: Evokes monsoon evenings — rain on rooftops, the smell of wet earth, romantic longing, the joy of homecoming. Light and accessible yet emotionally resonant.

western_equivalent.relationship: The Mixolydian mode (dominant scale) with its b7 matches Desh perfectly. Widely used in rock, blues, folk, and pop — giving Desh natural crossover potential.

lofi.notes: Mixolydian warmth + romantic mood = perfect lofi match. The komal Ni adds a blues tinge that pairs naturally with hip-hop influenced beats.

ambient.notes: Desh can work in ambient but its playful character prefers more rhythmic settings. Best for brighter, more uplifting ambient.

calm.notes: Naturally calm and warm. The Mixolydian brightness prevents heaviness while the komal Ni adds emotional depth. Perfect for relaxation.

upbeat.notes: Desh is the most naturally upbeat of the common ragas. Its bright major-ish scale and playful phrasing suit higher-energy production well.
